text: WorkOS Launches Auth.md: An Open Protocol for Agents to Register for Services on the Web. > Auth.md enables AI agents to automatically register for services on behalf of users through a standardized Markdown file and HTTP endpoints, solving the problem of agent authentication without browser-based OAuth flows. The protocol supports two main flows: > Agent verified: Agent provider/trusted platform attests to the user's identity. The service verifies it and returns credentials. > User claimed: The agent registers first, then the user claims it with an OTP workos.com/blog/agent-registration-with-auth-md Source:

text: SEC Delays Tokenized Stock Exemption Plan Amid Concerns Over Unauthorized Equity Tokens According to Bloomberg, the U.S. SEC has delayed a planned exemption framework for tokenized stock trading. One key concern involves allowing the trading of third-party equity tokens issued without authorization from the underlying public companies. Former regulators also warned that ensuring shareholder rights such as dividends and voting on pseudonymous blockchain networks remains technically and legally challenging. — link

text: i am fighting demons not to buy back into dolphin. nonstop fomo since the minute i sold. its the launchcoin trade all over again. have refrained so far, need to find ways to continue refraining. the pull is so strong, its unreal. i knew i had a problem but the scope is bigger than i had thought. the rationale is that even if it 10x without me here, missing out is still better than failing to stick to protocol. if i cave now then i am back to square on, with zero agency and zero control over my actions. a leaf in the wind. this trade is poisoned for me either way. if i buy back and it goes well and i make hundreds of thousands of dollars i would be destined to lose it all in the aftermath. because i would be an individual with zero agency. and thats what trading is all about. control your emotions and exploit others who cannot. i am a strong believer of the theory that our NW always regresses to the base level of our process. if you make it by excessive risk taking you will lose it by excessive risk taking. live by the sword die by the sword. gambid once said to me that people have precisely the amount of money they deserve from markets, and variance always evens itself out over time. that sentence resonated deeply with me.

text: The Chinese government has announced severe penalties against major US stock trading platforms operating within China, confiscating all illegal gains. This could benefit centralized exchanges (CEXs) and on-chain US stock trading. China's Securities and Exchange Commission (CSRC) has cracked down on illegal cross-border activities by Tiger Brokers (NZ), Futu Securities (HK), and Longbridge Securities (HK). The three platforms are accused of unauthorized securities trading services, marketing, public fund sales, and futures brokerage in mainland China. The CSRC and eight other departments have issued a joint plan to ban overseas firms from offering account opening, trading, and fund transfer services, with a two-year cleanup period for existing illegal business. — link

text: According to ThePrint, India's MeitY has issued a blocking order against Polymarket and is moving to block Kalshi as soon as Friday, enforcing its ban on prediction market platforms still operating in India. The orders are expected under Section 69A of the IT Act, requiring ISPs to block access, while non-compliant intermediaries could face up to seven years in prison and fines. India's online gaming law, effective May 1, bans real-money online games, related advertising and financial transactions. Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, reported that Polymarket has appointed a representative in Japan and is preparing to lobby for the authorization of prediction markets, aiming for government approval by 2030. Polymarket sees Japan as a large untapped business opportunity, with Mike Eidlin leading its Japan efforts; the platform currently blocks Japan-based users from placing bets due to "regulatory requirements." — link

text: Wang Chun, co-founder of F2Pool, one of the largest Bitcoin mining pools, and commander of Fram2, will fly aboard Starship's first human interplanetary spaceflight mission to Mars. The two-year mission will explore beyond the Earth-Moon system before flying by Mars and returning to Earth. Before the interplanetary mission, Wang will join Dennis Tito and Akiko Tito on the first planned Starship commercial human spaceflight around the Moon, a week-long circumlunar fly-by planned to pass within 200 km of the lunar surface to advance Starship's systems for deep-space, long-duration missions. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip CZ: Asian Countries Will Likely Build Bitcoin Reserves Under the Radar (ARCHIVE FOOTAGE) Binance founder CZ stated that cultural mindsets will likely lead Asian countries to build their Bitcoin reserves quietly. He dismissed the misconception that crypto disrupts traditional finance, noting instead that banks will become obsolete if they ignore this new technology. He also pointed out that crypto is far more transparent and easier to trace than traditional finance, with a much lower rate of illicit transactions. — link

text: Zerion's L2 (Zero Network) shuts down ZERØ Network is shutting down its gasless L2 rollup, citing that while the vision to eliminate gas fees for better onchain UX was correct, resources are better focused on Zerion's wallet and API products

text: Glassnode research shows that 6.04 million BTC, worth about $469 billion, have public keys exposed on-chain, accounting for 30.2% of issued supply and theoretically facing future quantum computing attack risks. The exposed supply includes 1.92 million BTC in structural exposure and 4.12 million BTC in operational exposure, the latter mainly due to address reuse. Exchanges account for 1.66 million BTC, or 8.3% of total supply and about 40% of all operationally unsafe BTC. — link
